author: spencer_tb
content:
I think it would be useful for us to discuss async what we want to include in the next Glamsterdam denvet before ACD this week. The remaining EIPs are as followed from what I can see (maybe I missed some):
- EIP-7610: https://forkcast.org/eips/7610
- EIP-7997: https://forkcast.org/eips/7997
- EIP-8070: https://forkcast.org/eips/8070
- EIP-7954 changes: https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/pull/11540
Assuming we have a stable `bal-denvet-7` with the new 8037 changes, I wouldn't be against adding all of these.

From what I understand these are more uncertain atm:
- EIP-7904: https://forkcast.org/eips/7904
- EIP-8038:  https://forkcast.org/eips/8038
- EIP-2780: https://forkcast.org/eips/2780

What do we want?

author: misilva73
content:
More context on the remaining repricing EIPs:
- Final numbers on 7904 are still pending on an investigation from Erigon. Based on their analysis, we may not need to increase any prices for compute operations. Will update on Monday, during ACDT
- 8038 and 2780 have a couple interactions between each other. So, I think it makes sense to add both EIPs at the same time. At the same time, they also interact with 8037, so to be cautious, I think we should add them sooner rather than later so that we have time to properly test and implement. We don't yet final numbers for 8038, but an option there would be to add both EIPs with as costs as they are so that we derisk the implementation and them simply update values when we have them. I am tending towards adding these two EIPs for the next Glamsterdam devnet, but happy to hear more opinions

author: misilva73
content: I have PRs to update both [8038](https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/pull/11623) and [2780](https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/pull/11645) so that the parameters work well between each other and with 8037. I can merge them this week.
